name: explain-code
description: Explains code with visual diagrams and analogies. Use when explaining how code works, teaching about a codebase, or when the user asks "how does this work?"
---

When explaining code, always include:

1. **Start with an analogy**: Compare the code to something from everyday life
2. **Draw a diagram**: Use ASCII art to show the flow, structure, or relationships
3. **Walk through the code**: Explain step-by-step what happens
4. **Highlight a gotcha**: What's a common mistake or misconception?

Keep explanations conversational. For complex concepts, use multiple analogies.

plugins.name: munin-memory
plugins.description:
# Munin for Claude Code

> **Munin** is a long-term memory system for developers. Every time you work with Claude Code, Munin remembers your context, decisions, bugs, and solutions — so you never have to repeat yourself.

**Munin** is your project's memory companion, integrated directly into Claude Code. It remembers your project, finds past errors, and suggests solutions based on what you've done before.

---

## Features

### 🤖 Munin Agent
Call `@munin` in any conversation to talk directly with your project's memory. Munin has full access to your memories — search, retrieve, store — all following the Memory Index Protocol.

### 🔍 Automatic Memory Search
Every time you start a Claude Code session, Munin automatically finds relevant memories from your project. No need to ask — context is already there.

### 💾 Session Summarization
When a session ends, Munin automatically saves a summary to memory. Tasks, decisions, bugs — everything is recorded.

### 📚 Error Catalog
When an error occurs, Munin will:
1. **Search** the error catalog first — a fix might already exist
2. **Fix** following the precedent
3. **Store** the new resolution so the next time is faster

### 🧠 GraphRAG Memory
Every memory is automatically indexed into a knowledge graph:
- **Entities** — services, libraries, models are extracted
- **Relationships** — connections between components are mapped
- **Semantic search** — find anything using natural language

---

## How It Works

```
You open Claude Code
        │
        ▼
  SessionStart Hook
  → Munin searches relevant memories
  → Context injected into conversation
        │
        ▼
  You work on your task
  → Munin monitors for errors
  → Errors → Error Catalog
        │
        ▼
  Session ends (or /compact)
  → Summary saved to memory
        │
        ▼
  Next session — Munin remembers
```

---

## Quick Start

### 1. Create a Free Munin Account

Sign up at [munin.kalera.app](https://munin.kalera.app) — FREE FOREVER.

After signing up, create a new project and copy your **Project ID** (`proj_xxxxxxxxxxxx`).

### 2. Add Your Project ID

Create or edit `.env` in your project root:

```bash
MUNIN_PROJECT=proj_your_project_id
```

### 3. Enable the MCP Server

**Option A — Global (recommended, works in any project):**
```bash
# Download the Munin MCP wrapper
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/3d-era/munin/main/scripts/munin-mcp-wrapper.sh \
  -o ~/.claude/munin-mcp-wrapper.sh
chmod +x ~/.claude/munin-mcp-wrapper.sh

# Enable globally
claude mcp add --scope user munin bash ~/.claude/munin-mcp-wrapper.sh
```

**Option B — Project-specific:**
```bash
claude mcp add --scope project munin bash -c '
  ENV_FILE="$(pwd)/.env"
  if [[ -f "$ENV_FILE" ]]; then
    while IFS="=" read -r key rest; do
      [[ "$key" =~ ^[[:space:]]*# ]] && continue
      [[ -z "$key" ]] && continue
      key=$(echo "$key" | sed "s/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//")
      value=$(echo "$rest" | sed "s/^[[:space:]]*//;s/#.*$//;s/[[:space:]]*$//")
      [[ -n "$value" ]] && export "$key=$value" 2>/dev/null || true
    done < "$ENV_FILE"
  fi
  exec npx @kalera/munin-claude mcp
'
```

**Option C — From this repo (development):**
```bash
claude mcp add --scope user munin bash -c '
  PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-$(pwd)}"
  ENV_FILE="$PROJECT_DIR/.env"
  if [[ -f "$ENV_FILE" ]]; then
    grep -E "^MUNIN_PROJECT=" "$ENV_FILE" | while IFS="=" read -r key val; do
      export "$key=$val"
    done
  fi
  exec npx @kalera/munin-claude mcp
'
```

### 4. Start Using

```bash
# Talk to Munin directly
@munion ask about the SePay integration
@munion what did we decide about the auth system?

# Or just start working — Munin auto-loads context
# Munin will remember everything when you finish
```

---

## Configuration

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `MUNIN_PROJECT` | *(required)* | Your project ID from munin.kalera.app |
| `MUNIN_BASE_URL` | `https://munin.kalera.dev` | Munin API server |
| `MUNIN_API_KEY` | *(from dashboard)* | API key for authentication |

### Self-hosted Munin

If you're running your own Munin instance:

```bash
MUNIN_BASE_URL=https://your-munin-instance.com \
claude mcp add --scope user munin bash -c \
  'MUNIN_BASE_URL="https://your-munin-instance.com" exec npx @kalera/munin-claude mcp'
```

---

## Skills — What Triggers Munin

Munin activates automatically based on context:

| Skill | Triggers | What It Does |
|-------|----------|--------------|
| `munin-memory` | "search memory", "remember", "find in memory" | Full memory operations |
| `munin-architecture` | "architecture", "tech stack", "system design" | Architecture context |
| `munin-error-catalog` | "bug", "error", "crash", "doesn't work" | Error resolution |

---

## Memory Protocol — How Munin Works

### At Task Start
1. `munin_search_memories` with keywords relevant to the task
2. `munin_retrieve_memory` for detailed context
3. Present findings — **verify from memory, don't guess**

### At Task End
`munin_store_memory` with:
- **title**: Concise summary (max 80 chars)
- **content**: Details including file paths, decisions, outcomes
- **tags**: Categories (`task`, `architecture`, `bug-fix`, etc.)

### On Bug Fixes
1. Search error catalog first
2. Present existing fix if found
3. Fix and **store the new resolution**

### On Decisions
Tag as `architecture` + `decision`, include timeline/version

plugins.name: netsuite-aiconnector-service-skill
plugins.description:
netsuite-ai-connector-instructions skill enables AI models to safely and intelligently interact with live NetSuite data through the MCP (Model Context Protocol).
It enforces structured decision-making for tool selection, ensuring the AI always prioritizes standard reports, saved searches, and native record operations before using custom queries. The skill embeds deep NetSuite domain knowledge, including transaction lifecycles, accounting logic, and multi-subsidiary handling.
It also applies strict guardrails for SuiteQL execution, output formatting, and data safety, ensuring responses are accurate, compliant, and business-ready.
Key capabilities include:

Intelligent tool orchestration (Reports → Saved Searches → Records → SuiteQL)
Built-in NetSuite domain expertise (financials, transactions, entities)
Multi-subsidiary and multi-currency awareness
Safe and governed SuiteQL execution with enforced limits
Standardized financial formatting and hyperlink generation
Error handling and recovery patterns aligned with NetSuite workflows
This skill transforms general-purpose AI into a NetSuite-aware operational assistant capable of delivering reliable, decision-grade insights and actions.

plugins.name: netsuite-sdf-roles-and-permissions
plugins.description:
netsuite-sdf-roles-and-permissions skill enables AI models to safely and accurately generate, review, and validate NetSuite SDF permission configurations for roles, script deployments, and integration designs. It enforces structured permission selection by requiring the AI to confirm exact NetSuite permission IDs from authoritative reference data rather than relying on UI labels or assumptions.
The skill embeds NetSuite domain knowledge around role design, least-privilege access, run-as role strategy, and SDF permission modeling. It helps ensure permission recommendations are precise, minimal, and production-safe, while also identifying when a permission should be treated as a standard NetSuite permission versus a project-specific customrecord_*script ID.
It also applies strict review rules for permission validity, duplication, level selection, and related access dependencies, ensuring outputs are accurate, governed, and implementation-ready.
Key capabilities include:
· Intelligent permission validation using exact NetSuite internal permission IDs
· Built-in expertise for SDF custom roles, script deployments, and run-as role design
· Clear distinction between standard permissions and custom record script IDs
· Least-privilege permlevel recommendations across VIEW, CREATE, EDIT, and FULL
· Validation against reference data for exact permkey accuracy
· Review guidance for duplicate permissions, missing dependencies, and unsupported assumptions
· Output patterns that include caveats, related permissions, and confidence when inference is required
This skill transforms general-purpose AI into a NetSuite-aware permissions assistant capable of producing reliable, least-privilege, implementation-grade guidance for SDF and role configuration workflows.

plugins.name: netsuite-uif-spa-reference
plugins.description:
netsuite-uif-spa-reference skill enables AI models to accurately build, modify, and debug NetSuite UIF (User Interface Framework) single-page application (SPA) components using authoritative API and type definitions from @uif-js/core and @uif-js/component.
It enforces precise component usage by guiding the AI to reference exact constructors, props, enums, hooks, and lifecycle behaviors, ensuring that generated UI code aligns with NetSuite’s SPA framework standards and avoids common runtime failures.
The skill embeds deep UIF domain expertise, including component composition patterns, state management, event handling, and layout strategies, along with production-proven best practices and known pitfalls. It helps prevent issues such as invalid component usage, improper state updates, rendering errors, and layout inconsistencies.
It also applies strict guardrails around component configuration, ensuring correct use of writable vs constructor-only properties, proper data binding patterns, and safe handling of asynchronous operations within UIF lifecycle hooks.
Key capabilities include:
· Accurate API lookup for @uif-js/core and @uif-js/component classes, methods, and enums
· Built-in UIF framework expertise for SPA component design and architecture
· Guidance on layout patterns (StackPanel, GridPanel, DataGrid, Modal, etc.)
· Safe state management using hooks, Store, and immutable update patterns
· Debugging support for runtime errors, rendering issues, and invalid component usage
· Enforcement of correct DataGrid configuration, editing behavior, and event handling
· Prevention of common pitfalls (StackPanel null children, Modal placement, UIF Date handling, etc.)
· Best practices for async operations, cancellation handling, and lifecycle-safe updates
This skill transforms general-purpose AI into a NetSuite UIF-aware frontend assistant capable of producing reliable, production-ready SPA components and resolving complex UI framework issues.

plugins.name: nova-thesis
plugins.description:
nova-thesis challenges technical implementations before they ship. Present any implementation — a problem statement, a design sketch, a code snippet, or a full repository — and the skill refuses to accept it at
face value. It scores the work across five dimensions (Correctness, Completeness, Scalability, Security, Maintainability), surfaces a real-world incident from an index of public postmortems that matches the
riskiest gap, and asks hard, specific questions anchored to that incident. It keeps pushing until every dimension scores at least 7/10, or until you consciously name and accept the remaining trade-offs.

Unlike review tools that confirm, nova-thesis is built to find where things break — before production does. Read-only, MIT licensed, no telemetry, no external services required. Ships with 20 test scenarios and
a defended-thesis example that proves the skill also correctly approves strong work.

  - creating PowerPoint presentations in pptx
  - creating Word documents in docx
  - creating structured worksheets in xlsx
  - checking whether a requested Office workflow is supported before execution

  The plugin packages a Claude Code skill and routes supported Office-file tasks to the user's local officecli installation. It is designed for users who want Claude-assisted Office document workflows while keeping execution on their own
  machine.

plugins.name: oh-my-forge
plugins.description:
oh-my-forge is a Claude Code workflow harness that prevents agent mistakes from repeating. Built on everything-claude-code, it adds an ontology-driven structural error       
  prevention system: every agent failure is classified, routed, and converted into a permanent constraint so the same error cannot structurally happen again. It also includes a
   Codex delegation system where Claude uses an ontology index as a GPS — reading only a domain entry and a spec doc (~3K tokens) instead of the full source tree, then handing
  a structured BRIEF to Codex for implementation. Includes 125+ skills, specialized agents, slash commands, and automated hooks for TDD, code review, security scanning, and    
  continuous learning.

plugins.name: olostep
plugins.description:
Turn any website into clean, LLM-ready markdown or structured data. Olostep integrates powerful web scraping, crawling, search, and AI-powered answers directly into Claude Code. Scrape single pages, crawl entire sites, map site structures, batch-process thousands of URLs, and search the web — all with automatic JavaScript rendering, anti-bot bypasses, and real-time extraction built in.
The plugin includes an AI answers tool that goes beyond scraping: describe what you need in plain language and Olostep searches the live web, synthesizes the results, and returns a source-backed answer with structured JSON output — no URLs required.
How to use: After installing, set your OLOSTEP_API_KEY environment variable. Then use these commands to get started:
/olostep:scrape — Extract any webpage as clean markdown, HTML, or structured JSON.
/olostep:crawl — Crawl and extract content from an entire website.
/olostep:search — Search the web and get deduplicated results with titles and descriptions.
/olostep:map — Discover all URLs on a site instantly.
/olostep:answer — Ask a question in plain language and get a source-backed answer from live web data.
/olostep:batch — Scrape up to 10,000 URLs in a single async job.
You can also use Olostep's tools directly in conversation: ask Claude to scrape a URL, search for recent information, extract structured data with a custom schema, or research competitors across multiple sites.

plugins.name: onboard
plugins.description: End-to-end Claude tooling generator for existing codebases. Performs deep codebase analysis (languages, frameworks, conventions, complexity), walks you through an interactive wizard, then generates a complete suite of Claude artifacts — CLAUDE.md files, path-scoped rules, project-specific skills and agents, hook configuration, PR templates, and commit conventions. Unlike recommendation-only tools, onboard produces actual files tailored to your project's detected patterns and enforced configs. Includes drift detection via /onboard:evolve to keep tooling aligned as your codebase changes. Also serves as the generation engine for the companion forge plugin (new projects) via headless invocation.

plugins.name: oneconnecter-plugin
plugins.description: Real-time data for AI agents through intelligent semantic routing. Access weather, cryptocurrency prices, stock market data, commodities, and company intelligence. Instead of overwhelming Claude with dozens of tools, OneConnecter routes queries to specialized data agents - right source, every time. Features semantic caching (78% token reduction), sub-second routing, and multi-intent query splitting. Perfect for financial research, market monitoring, and any workflow needing live external data.

Written in Rust this is a single binary, no runtime dependencies.

When any company's state evolves in a way that matches your pattern, OpenFunnel fires an event instantly - piped directly to Slack or your CRM with the right people behind the change and what happened.

OpenFunnel is built to detect leading indicators of pain in real-time.

Automated academic paper review system for Claude Code. Integrates PDF parsing (marker-pdf + PyMuPDF), reference

  Features venue-adaptive review strategies for 15+ top CS conferences (CVPR, NeurIPS, ICML, ICLR, ECCV, ICCV, ACL,
  AAAI, KDD, MICCAI, etc.), powered by distilled methodologies from 10 senior conference reviewers. Includes
  8-dimension scoring, statistical rigor checks, automated quick assessment, rebuttal assistance, and parallel
  agent-based review architecture.

  Components: 3 commands (/review-paper, /pre-screen, /rebuttal), 4 specialized agents, 1 skill with 5 reference files,
   2 MCP servers (11 tools total), automated dependency checking, and a quick assessment script.

plugins.name: parallax

Parallax is an autopilot for long, complex tasks in Claude Code. Append `parallaxthink` to any prompt, and at the end of each turn Parallax spawns an advisory agent in an isolated context,
finds regions the mission implicitly required but the agent never addressed, and surfaces one back into the turn. The loop continues until the task is flawlessly done — turning a single
prompt into the equivalent of several manual follow-ups, without any extra effort from you.

Works with Claude Code's plugin system — install via claude plugin marketplace add animeshpatni94/parrot then claude plugin install parrot@parrot. Stacks with other skills like caveman for up to 80% token reduction.

plugins.name: pearl-llm-memory
plugins.description:
Externalized Cognitive State Transfer

PEARL-LLM Memory (.pearl) is a JSON-based file format for storing structured, layered, verifiable context that persists across agent sessions. It gives autonomous agents a durable memory with immutable history, temperature-graded recall, cryptographic chain of custody, and branching decision trails.

The format is agent-agnostic. The reference implementation is a zero-dependency Python CLI. Both are open.

Unlike single-pass planning, it forces fundamentally different work at each stage: the explore phase rejects plan headings, the critique phase rejects finalization attempts. plugins.name: plausibleba-business-architecture-skills
plugins.description:
Methodology-grade business architecture for Claude. Four slash commands that elicit, validate, and visualise a complete business architecture from any business description, no templates or prior models needed.

/plausibleba - Guided session producing a Capability Map, Concept Model, and Value Stream in one flow. Exports a single ba-skills-bundle.json.

/capability-map - BIZBOK-grounded MECE capability hierarchy (L1–L3) with interactive treemap. Auto-generates XLSX and JSON exports.

/concept-model - Business object taxonomy using the Capsicum Triad (Party/Record/Resource). Cross-validates against capability coverage.

/value-stream - Staged value delivery with outcome chains, PPIT breakdown, and capability alignment.

Each skill includes two validation checkpoints before rendering, auto-generated XLSX/JSON downloads, and recommended next steps to run companion skills. 

Open source at github.com/plausibleba/ba-skills.

plugins.name: playcraft

plugins.name: playlist-curator

plugins.name: playwright-pro
plugins.description:
Playwright Pro turns your AI coding agent into a senior test automation engineer.

Instead of generating tutorial-quality tests with brittle CSS selectors, Playwright Pro enforces battle-tested patterns from production test suites: role-based locators, web-first assertions, proper fixture architecture, and isolated test design.

It doesn't reinvent Claude Code — it orchestrates it. /pw:generate uses the built-in Explore subagent for codebase analysis. /pw:migrate delegates to /batch for parallel file conversion. /pw:fix leverages /debug for trace inspection. Every skill builds on what Claude Code already does well.

55 ready-to-use test templates across 11 categories — authentication, CRUD, checkout, search, forms, dashboard, settings, onboarding, notifications, API testing, and accessibility. Each template includes TypeScript and JavaScript variants with parametrizable placeholders. Not generic stubs — real patterns like Stripe iframe handling, GraphQL mutation testing, and RBAC permission flows.

3 specialized agents handle complex planning before a single test is written: test-architect maps your application surface, test-debugger diagnoses flaky failures systematically, and migration-planner creates ordered conversion plans from Cypress or Selenium.

2 MCP integrations (TypeScript) connect to TestRail (8 tools) and BrowserStack (7 tools) for teams that need test management and cross-browser coverage. Both are optional — the plugin works fully without them.

Smart hooks auto-validate every test file you write (catches waitForTimeout, hardcoded URLs, non-web-first assertions) and auto-detect Playwright projects on session start. Zero overhead — they run silently.

plugins.name: please-optimize-my-claude
plugins.description: Optimize your CLAUDE.md hierarchy — deduplicate content, detect misplacement, compress bloat, and visualize token usage across global/project/repo levels.

Works with 35+ components (Button, Input, Select, Sidebar, Menu, Dialog, Tabs, DatePicker, and more), 14 hooks, and 460+ icon subpath imports. Auto-invokes when your project imports from @plexui/ui.

  Search across plugin names, descriptions, and skill names. Filter by source (Official/Community) and status. Explore the marketplace with  available plugins. Features dark mode, keyboard shortcuts, toast notifications, SSE live updates, and secure localhost-only auth.

plugins.name: pm-skills
plugins.description: PM-Skills is an open-source collection of 27 product management skills for Claude Code. It covers the full product lifecycle — from discovery and problem definition through delivery and iteration — using the Triple Diamond framework. Each skill includes structured instructions, professional templates, and real-world examples so Claude produces consistent, high-quality PM artifacts instead of generic responses. Includes 3 workflow bundles (Triple Diamond, Lean Startup, Feature Kickoff) and an interactive skill builder. Apache 2.0 licensed, follows the agentskills.io specification.

plugins.name: pmm
plugins.description:
If you have ever had AI hallucinate and make things up (and  gaslight you, even): it's because summaries do not preserve nuance.

Each /compact cycle summarises and compresses. Decisions lose their rationale. Lessons lose their context. By Monday, you're re-explaining choices you made on Thursday. That's context rot. 

PMM fixes that. Every session, Claude loads what it already knows, then adds to it. 

Markdown files, git-backed. No database, no setup. Decisions tracked with rationale: not just what but. git log as your memory audit trail.

Memory that compounds, not rots.

Every review includes severity-coded feedback (from minor polish to hiring blockers), plus a clear hiring signal and level calibration.

If your portfolio isn’t landing interviews, this will tell you why and what to fix first.

Export your results as a clean HTML report to share or track progress over time.

plugins.name: pramana
plugins.description:
Reference implementation of the semantic layer from "Knowledge
  Engineering: The Future of AI-Assisted Software Engineering" (https://kn
  owledgeengineering.substack.com/p/knowledge-engineering-the-future).
  Turns a directory of Markdown files into a queryable knowledge graph
  with four primitives — get, search, traverse, and list. Domain experts
  encode business knowledge as interconnected artifacts; AI agents access
  it through structured retrieval instead of unstructured context dumps.
  Includes three skills: /pramana:setup for daemon lifecycle,
  /pramana:query for token-efficient semantic retrieval, and
  /pramana:author for creating artifacts with auto-elicited author
  profiles. Supports multi-tenant serving of multiple knowledge bases from
   a single daemon.

plugins.name: preflight

plugins.name: prepkit-product
plugins.description:
Product management skills for Claude Code. Gives Claude structured methodologies for discovery synthesis (JTBD, empathy mapping, journey mapping), user research design    
  (interview guides, screeners, synthesis plans), opportunity mapping (pursue/monitor/defer decisions), PRD authoring (evidence-linked requirements, Given/When/Then scenarios),
   prioritization (RICE, MoSCoW, ICE scoring), metrics analysis (North Star, metric trees, HEART framework), engagement design (habit loops, gamification, PLG flows), and      
  validation (evidence grading, cheapest-next-evidence routing). Each skill enforces evidence quality gates and chains into the next logical product step.

plugins.name: preserve-session
plugins.description:
Preserves Claude Code session history across project directory renames, moves, and copies by assigning each project a path-independent UUID and maintaining a global registry mapping UUID to current path.

Addresses a long-standing pain point: --resume and --continue silently lose session history when paths change. Closes anthropics/claude-code#5768; also tracked in #38089, #28745, #26766, #27883.

Commands: /preserve-session:fix recovers sessions after rename/move (with copy detection and automatic .jsonl merge on destination collision), /preserve-session:inherit copies session history from another registered project, /preserve-session:doctor diagnoses hash/registry state and slug collisions including non-ASCII path issues on macOS, /preserve-session:uninstall removes all preserve-session data with a preview-then-confirm safety pattern. A SessionStart hook auto-initializes .claude/hash.txt on first run — zero manual setup.

  Demo: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lWMrzASG7_o

  Quality signals: 24 verified test scenarios (rename, move, copy, slug collision, non-ASCII paths, registry corruption recovery, full --resume E2E). Atomic registry writes via fcntl.LOCK_EX + tempfile.mkstemp + os.replace. NFC slug normalization to match Claude Code's path encoding on macOS. Python heredoc isolation against shell injection. Symlink-safe uninstall (isinstance/dict guards).
  Shared helpers in hooks/common.sh with cross-platform Python discovery and uuidgen fallback.

  Full design rationale, security notes, and test plan are in two related submissions:
  • https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/pull/39148 (initial bundled-plugin attempt; comprehensive design + 24-item test plan with collapsible sections)
  • https://github.com/anthropics/claude-plugins-official/pull/1347 (auto-closed by submission bot; retained as reference for the proposed marketplace.json entry)

  Plugin location: lives at plugins/preserve-session/ inside the wonbywondev/claude-plugins monorepo. The marketplace entry should use the git-subdir source format pointing to that subdirectory.

plugins.name: product-review-expert
plugins.description:
product-review-expert is a Claude Code plugin for structured product and UX review. It is intended to help users analyze product requirement documents, workflow descriptions, page plans, and interaction designs before implementation or release.

The plugin focuses on surfacing concrete quality issues rather than generating generic feedback. Its review scope includes workflow completeness, state-transition coverage, exception and edge-case handling, role and permission consistency, rule conflicts, action-path friction, missing feedback, and weak error-state design.

The repository currently includes:

3 skills:
product-audit
logic-defect-review
interaction-review
1 agent:
senior-product-reviewer
1 local hook:
a Stop hook that runs a bundled local script for lightweight normalization/logging purposes
The plugin does not depend on external APIs, hosted services, MCP servers, OAuth flows, or user-provided secrets. It does not send user content over the network. The included hook only executes a local bundled script and writes a timestamped diagnostic log under ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}. It does not modify user repository files or perform analytics collection.

The plugin is documented with:

a public GitHub repository
a manifest in .claude-plugin/plugin.json
installation and usage instructions in README.md
semantic versioning and changelog
license information
Marketplace-facing usage is documented with namespaced commands such as:

/product-review-expert:product-audit
/product-review-expert:logic-defect-review
/product-review-expert:interaction-review
/product-review-expert:senior-product-reviewer
The intended users are product managers, designers, business analysts, and teams reviewing process-heavy or stateful product designs such as approval systems, admin panels, internal tools, B2B workflows, and e-commerce flows.

plugins.name: railguard
plugins.description:
Railguard is a safer, context-aware alternative to --dangerously-skip-permissions for Claude Code.

Instead of choosing between approving every command manually or giving Claude unrestricted access, Railguard sits in the middle. It intercepts every tool call — Bash, Write, Edit, Read — and makes policy-based decisions: allow safe commands instantly, block dangerous ones automatically, and ask for human approval when it's not sure.

Out of the box, Railguard comes with 29 default rules covering destructive operations like terraform destroy, rm -rf, DROP TABLE, and git force-push. It detects evasion attempts (base64 encoding, chr() construction, variable substitution, shell wrapping), restricts file access with path fencing (blocking ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, /etc), snapshots every file write for one-click rollback, and logs every decision to a structured audit trail.

Everything is customizable through a simple railguard.yaml file in your project directory.

matches against what you have on hand.
3. Meal Planning — Generate weekly meal plans from your saved recipes, balancing variety and difficulty across the week. Also produces nutritional summaries.
4. Grocery Lists — Consolidate ingredients from recipes or meal plans into a shopping list, grouped by store section with duplicates combined.

What It Does

Analyses your positioning against the role
Extracts recruiter keywords and builds a strategic placement map
Generates a tailored resume (.docx) with precise formatting

this plugin helps user screen and rank job candidates by scoring resumes against a job description — consistently, at scale.

What It Does

This plugin adds a shortlisting skill to Claude that automates the most time-consuming parts of hiring:

Reads your job description and builds a structured evaluation rubric (once, reused forever)
Scores each resume across four dimensions: Skills, Experience, Education, and Fit
Applies hard rejection rules to instantly filter obviously unqualified candidates

plugins.name: security-watchdog
plugins.description:
> Automatic security scanner for Claude Code plugins — detects new or updated extensions at session start and scans them for prompt injection, malicious hook scripts, and data exfiltration.

## The Problem

LLM extension ecosystems are a novel attack surface. Traditional security tooling does not cover two threats unique to AI extensions:

- **Host attacks** — hook scripts run shell commands automatically on session start, with full user privileges
- **Mind attacks** — skill and command files are injected directly into the model's reasoning context, enabling prompt injection

No existing security scanner understands these threat classes. Security Watchdog is purpose-built for them.

## How It Works

```
You run: /plugin install someone/new-plugin
                    ↓
Next session starts (SessionStart hook fires)
                    ↓
check-new-plugins.sh diffs installed_plugins.json
against a snapshot from the previous session
                    ↓
New/updated plugin detected
                    ↓
Injects into session context:
  "SECURITY WATCHDOG ALERT: forge@forge was installed.
   Run /scan-plugin forge@forge before proceeding."
                    ↓
Claude runs /scan-plugin, reads all plugin files,
applies security checks, and reports findings
```

The snapshot is updated on detection — each change event fires the alert exactly once.

plugins.name: shift-leadership-skills-for-the-ai-era
plugins.description:
SHIFT: Leadership Skills for the AI Era is a personalised AI leadership development agent. It does not teach generic leadership content. It becomes your leadership partner by learning who you are, what you do, and who you are becoming.

When you install SHIFT, the first thing it does is learn your world: your role, your industry, your team, your biggest challenge, and your 12-month goal. From that point forward, every exercise, every reflection, every piece of development is specific to your life. A plumber running a crew of 6 gets different coaching than an HR director managing a call centre. Same skills. Different application. Because context changes everything.

SHIFT builds 6 essential skills:
S: Self-Management — regulating emotions, energy, and reactions under pressure
H: Human Intelligence — reading people, adapting approach, building trust
I: Innovation — questioning assumptions, taking initiative, proposing ideas
F: Focus — prioritising ruthlessly, aligning effort to outcomes
T: Thinking — independent reasoning, structured decisions, owning conclusions
Y: Your AI Edge — leading effectively in an AI-augmented workplace

The agent uses SHIFT Skill Signatures to tag every challenge you bring. Single codes (S, H, I, F, T) and compound codes (S+H, F+T, H+I, and 7 more) that reveal the real pattern behind your leadership challenges. Over time you start recognising your own patterns and thinking in SHIFT language.

What you get:
- Identity onboarding that personalises everything to your role and context
- 25-question diagnostic with scored skill profiles and compound code analysis
- 12-month development journey tied to your life and career goals
- Weekly micro-practices tailored to your actual work (5 minutes per day)
- Friday reflections with progress stats, improvement areas, and next-week focus
- Real-time coaching on any leadership challenge using the SHIFT Coaching Lens
- Personalised deliverables: energy maps, motivation maps, prioritisation matrices, decision journals, team discussion guides
- Gmail/Calendar integration for weekly rhythm reminders

The first 2 weeks are free with full access. Week 3 onwards requires an access code from leadershipbydesign.co/shift-access.

Built on 11 years of methodology from Leadership by Design. 4,000+ leaders developed across 30+ organisations and 25+ programmes.

Why use SHIFT instead of just asking AI about leadership? Raw AI gives you information. SHIFT gives you transformation. It knows your context, thinks in a proven system, tracks your development over time, produces deliverables you actually use, and builds a leadership operating system through daily practice that compounds over 12 months.

plugins.name: skill-provenance

When you save, it bumps versions, recomputes hashes, and updates the changelog. Works anywhere the agentskills.io standard does (30+ agent tools).

plugins.name: skill-safety-auditor

plugins.name: skill-security-check
plugins.description: Security audit plugin that scans Claude Code skills, hooks, and MCP configurations for threats. Detects 37 attack patterns including prompt injection, data exfiltration, supply chain attacks (ToxicSkills/ClawHavoc), credential theft, reverse shells, backdoor persistence, API endpoint hijacking, Unicode homoglyph attacks, and context window poisoning. Includes runtime hooks for Bash command validation and MCP response inspection.

plugins.name: skill-seekers

plugins.name: skillbase

Handles the full pipeline: feature engineering, synthetic data generation, model training, weight export, and TypeScript codegen. The resulting model runs server-side (Node.js/Next.js) or client-side (browser) in <1ms with a ~10-50 KB weights file. No Python runtime, no ONNX, no TensorFlow.js needed at runtime.

plugins.name: skyvern-browser-automation

plugins.name: slide-revive
plugins.description:
Rebuild NotebookLM slide PDFs as fully editable PPTX files using Vision AI.

NotebookLM exports each slide as a flat image, making text and shapes impossible to edit. slide-revive extracts each page, analyzes it with Claude Vision, reconstructs the slide structure with native text boxes, tables, and shapes, and generates a polished .pptx alongside an instant .html preview — all in one command. No external API key required. Supports macOS and Windows.

plugins.name: slipstream
plugins.description:
A tool that makes AI coding agents more effective for Dart and Flutter projects.

AI agents working on Dart and Flutter run into two recurring problems. First, their training data has a cutoff — they'll reach for discontinued packages, pin outdated major versions, or produce subtly wrong API signatures that compile only after a correction loop. Second, Flutter development is inherently visual and stateful, and agents have no way to see a running app: they can't observe a layout failure, verify that a state change took effect, or confirm that a navigation worked.

This tool addresses both. It adds hooks that catch stale package choices before they land in `pubspec.yaml`, an MCP server that retrieves accurate package API signatures directly from the local pub cache, and a suite of MCP commands for launching, inspecting, and interacting with a running Flutter app.

plugins.name: smart-compact
plugins.description: Save structured session context before /clear and auto-restore it next session. Smart Compact captures what was done, why, decisions made, problems solved, and next steps — so you never  lose conversation context again. Includes a SessionStart hook that auto-detects saved context and prompts to reload it.

plugins.name: smarthealthconnect
plugins.description:
SmartHealthConnect turns Claude into a patient-facing health copilot. Six auto-discoverable skills invoke themselves based on natural prompts:

• care-completion — tracks preventive screenings, referrals, and follow-ups against HEDIS and USPSTF quality measures. Triggers on "what screenings am I due for", "show my care gaps".
• medication-refills — monitors refill windows, requests refills, and projects 90-day timelines. Triggers on "refill my lisinopril", "which meds am I about to run out of".
• healthy-habits — a longitudinal health operating picture integrating sleep, exercise, medication adherence, vitals, and goal progress.
• diet-exercise — logs activity and meals and analyzes correlations between exercise/diet and clinical outcomes (BP, HbA1c, weight).
• kids-health — CDC immunization schedules, well-child visit cadence, and state-specific school health compliance.
• research-monitor — filters bioRxiv / medRxiv preprints, ClinicalTrials.gov, and OpenFDA drug-safety signals to the patient's actual conditions and medications.

Built on SMART on FHIR with PHI redaction, audit logging, and human-in-the-loop on write tools (add_journal_entry, generate_care_plan, generate_appointment_prep). Pairs cleanly with healthclaw-guardrails for the backend FHIR layer.

plugins.name: smb-cybersecurity-policy-kit
plugins.description: Generate NIST CSF 2.0-aligned cybersecurity policies for small and mid-size businesses. Five production-ready generators (Acceptable Use, Incident Response, Password & MFA, Data Classification, Remote Work Security) plus a policy-auditor agent that grades existing policies against NIST CSF 2.0. Built for SMBs, MSPs, and IT leaders who need defensible, audit-ready documentation without hiring a vCISO. Each policy is parameterized via XML variables (industry, size, risk profile, geography) so generated documents are tailored, not generic.

plugins.name: smb-hr-compliance-pack
plugins.description: Generate state-compliant HR documents for small and mid-size businesses. Five production-ready generators covering Offer Letter, Employee Handbook, Performance Improvement Plan, Termination Package (checklist + letter + paycheck guide), and 90-Day Onboarding Checklist. State-aware language (at-will, final-paycheck rules, leave laws) reduces legal risk for SMBs without an in-house HR team. Built for founders, office managers, and HR consultants who need defensible employment documents in minutes instead of days.

plugins.name: superfast
plugins.description:
You know that moment when you've finally convinced your coding agent to build exactly what you want, only for it to instantly get hopelessly lost hallucinating dependencies in a 5,000-line codebase?

Superfast fixes that. It starts by establishing your Context Memory. Before any brainstorming or planning occurs, it instantly triggers the FastMemory Build-Up Skill, recursively mapping your enterprise repository into a deterministic CBFDAE graph. Once your agent's cognitive map is established, it steps back and asks you what you're really trying to do.

Once it's teased a spec out of the conversation, it shows it to you in chunks short enough to actually read and digest. After you've signed off on the design, your agent puts together an implementation plan that's clear enough for an enthusiastic junior engineer with poor taste, no judgement, no project context, and an aversion to testing to follow. It emphasizes true red/green TDD, YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It), and DRY.

Next up, once you say "go", it launches a subagent-driven-development process, having agents work through each engineering task, inspecting and reviewing their work, and continuing forward. It's not uncommon for Claude to be able to work autonomously for a couple hours at a time without deviating from the plan you put together.

plugins.name: superglue
plugins.description: Build, test, deploy and integrate superglue tools via the sg CLI and superglue SDKs. Useful for viewing integrations, running integration tools, testing authentication and connection to external systems.

plugins.name: superpowers
plugins.description:
It starts from the moment you fire up your coding agent. As soon as it sees that you're building something, it doesn't just jump into trying to write code. Instead, it steps back and asks you what you're really trying to do.

Once it's teased a spec out of the conversation, it shows it to you in chunks short enough to actually read and digest.

After you've signed off on the design, your agent puts together an implementation plan that's clear enough for an enthusiastic junior engineer with poor taste, no judgement, no project context, and an aversion to testing to follow. It emphasizes true red/green TDD, YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It), and DRY.

Next up, once you say "go", it launches a subagent-driven-development process, having agents work through each engineering task, inspecting and reviewing their work, and continuing forward. It's not uncommon for Claude to be able to work autonomously for a couple hours at a time without deviating from the plan you put together.

There's a bunch more to it, but that's the core of the system. And because the skills trigger automatically, you don't need to do anything special. Your coding agent just has Superpowers.

plugins.name: superpowers-beads
plugins.description: Workflow skills that turn Claude Code into a disciplined software engineer. Brainstorm designs, decompose into task-tracked plans via Beads issue tracker, execute with TDD, and verify with multi-stage review — all within structured, attention-anchored workflows that prevent drift and scope creep.

plugins.name: superpowers-optimized
plugins.description:
Built on the trusted obra/superpowers workflow and refined through research into LLM agent behavior, it adds automatic 3-tier workflow routing, proactive safety hooks, self-consistency verification at critical decision points, cross-session memory, and adversarial red-teaming — everything the original does, plus the discipline layer it was missing.

Cross-session memory changes the experience fundamentally. Without it, every session starts blind: the AI re-explores structure it already mapped, re-proposes approaches that were already rejected, re-debugs errors that were already solved. With the four-file memory stack, it arrives knowing what was tried, what was decided, and why — and builds forward instead of sideways.

Five research-backed principles run throughout: *less is more* (minimal always-on instructions), *fresh context beats accumulated context* (subagents get clean scoped prompts, not polluted history), *compliance ≠ competence* (instructions must be carefully engineered, not just comprehensive), *verify your own reasoning* (multi-path self-consistency catches confident-but-wrong failures before they become expensive), and *accountability drives accuracy* (agents that know their output has real downstream consequences perform better).

plugins.name: supertool
plugins.description: Batch multiple file operations (read, grep, glob, ls, tail, head) into a single Bash round-trip. Instead of N separate Read/Grep/Glob tool calls — each re-paying the cached context prefix — one ./supertool call handles them all. Includes auto-promote (concrete glob becomes read, single-file grep includes full file), enforcement mode that blocks redundant built-in tools, and per-call logging with caller tracking. Python 3.6+, stdlib-only, 80 tests.

  manage environments, webhooks, and secrets.

plugins.name: tidy
plugins.description:
tidy provides an interactive TDD workflow powered by Kent Beck's Tidy First approach. You write a plan (a markdown checklist of small tasks), then execute them one by one through a strict Red → Green → Refactor cycle.

The plugin acts as a "Genie" — it executes your commands (go, refactor, commit) but never makes strategic decisions on its own. You stay in control of what to build, when to tidy, and when to commit.

Key features:

go: Write a failing test, then write the minimum code to pass it. One task at a time.
refactor: Apply one Tidy First pattern (Guard Clause, Extract Method, Rename, etc.) and verify tests still pass.
commit: Commit with strict separation — structural changes and behavioral changes never mix.
Built-in Tidy First patterns: 11 tidying patterns with prioritization (eliminate duplication → improve clarity → reduce complexity).
Context-constrained: Only loads the current task, never the full plan. Keeps the conversation focused.

plugins.name: total-recall
plugins.description:
Coding Agents forget everything when a session ends. total-recall fixes that: a shared memory layer that persists across sessions, tools, and devices.
 
Memory is organized into three tiers (Hot / Warm / Cold) with a 4,000-token budget enforced automatically — so Claude gets relevant context injected at session start without carrying stale noise. Semantic search (all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embeddings, offline) retrieves the right memories on demand.
 
 A knowledge base lets you ingest READMEs, API docs, and architecture notes that are retrieved semantically when relevant. An eval framework lets you measure retrieval quality, run benchmarks, and compare config changes — so you know memory is actually helping.
 
All state is local: SQLite + ONNX embeddings, no external services, no API keys required.
 
Works across Claude Code, Copilot CLI, Cursor, Cline, and OpenCode — existing memories auto-import on first run.
